intro: Amplitude and Nivo both show up under the product analytics category, but they solve slightly different jobs. Amplitude uses pricing that starts at $61/mo (Plus) with free tier, while Nivo runs on a free model. Lock-in is medium for Amplitude and low for Nivo. Transparency lands at 4/5 versus 5/5. Amplitude fits teams working on enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts, while Nivo is a closer match when the job is automated build/test/deploy pipelines. Worth noting: Amplitude is explicitly not for startup with <1K users; Nivo is explicitly not for marketing-only analytics or pure content sites. The honest trade-off: Amplitude trades off on startup with <1K users — PostHog free tier is more generous; Nivo trades off on steeper learning curve for non-technical stakeholders. On the plus side, Amplitude highlights enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts, while Nivo points to deep funnel and cohort analysis for product decisions. Amplitude's documentation also calls out warehouse-native mode (Snowflake/BigQuery).
quick_take: Amplitude is for enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts; Nivo is for automated build/test/deploy pipelines; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Amplitude if your project is enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts, medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off.
verdict_b: Choose Nivo if your project is automated build/test/deploy pipelines, you want to keep future migration cheap.

faq.a: Amplitude uses pricing that starts at $61/mo (Plus) with free tier, and Nivo uses a free model. The pricing models are different, so a direct cheaper-than comparison depends on your volume and usage pattern.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Amplitude to Nivo?
faq.a: Our data puts Amplitude at medium lock-in (proprietary data model, warehouse-native reduces lock-in), and Nivo at low lock-in (oss react charting library). Migration is feasible but not trivial — budget time for re-integration, data export, and parallel running before cutover.
Both score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis. Nivo does edge ahead on pricing/docs transparency (5/5 vs 4/5), which can make evaluation faster.

faq.q: Is Nivo a good alternative to Amplitude?
faq.a: Nivo is a reasonable alternative to Amplitude when your workload leans more toward product teams tracking user behavior and retention than enterprise. The pricing model shifts too — Amplitude is pricing that starts at $61/mo (Plus) with free tier, Nivo is a free model — so expect the cost profile to change as well. One caveat: Nivo is explicitly not for marketing-only analytics or pure content sites, so check that constraint against your use-case before switching.
